title: Cryopreservation of labyrinthulomycetes in trehalose
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6sqk9","text":"This method adapted from instructions provided by Daiske Honda, Konan University. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: mix 20% glycerol 10% trehalose in ~35ppt artifical seawater Autoclave

For Aurantiochytrium and Schizochytrium, we've grown cultures in 790By+ (or 1/2 790By+) to late-log or early stationary phase. For Oblongichytrium, we've grown cells in GPY to late-log or early stationary phase. We have not experimented with how growth phases affects cryopreservation success with these strains. We are still testing Aplanochytrium and Labyrinthula.
Mix gently 1 volume of cell culture with 1 volume cryopreservant solution (final concentration 10% glycerol, 5% trehalose) We usually mix 0.5 ml culture with 0.5 cryo solution in 2 ml cryovials
Place cyrovials in Mr Frosty (filled with isopropanol as instructed) Place Mr Frosty in -80 freezer overnight
Transfer cryovials to a -80 freezer for storage

Thaw at 30C for 3-5 min, until just melted Pour into ~10 ml growth medium (such as 790) Return to standard growth conditions Sometimes cultures growth is evident the next day; sometimes it takes nearly a week.
creator: Jackie Collier
